content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 5, 1990


Honorable Gerald N. Felando
Assemblymember, 51st Assembly District
P.O. Box 4198
Torrance, CA 95510

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-087

Dear Assemblymember Felando:

This is in response to your request for a confirmation of
telephone advice regarding your filing responsibilities under the
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

This letter confirms that your letter dated January 30, 1990
accurately summarizes the telephone advice I provided to you on
January 29, 1990 concerning the amendment of your campaign state-
ments to reflect a loan you received in 1988 from another
Assemblymember, which you would like to repay.

As I stated in our telephone conversation, generally where a
candidate lists a payment received as a contribution, the Commis-
sion will not permit the repayment of the contribution to the
contributor. This is especially true under the new provisions of
the Act enacted by Proposition 73 which prohibit the transfer of
campaign contributions between candidates. (Section 85304.)

However, you stated in our telephone conversation that the
maker of the loan in fact disclosed the payment as a loan on his
campaign disclosure statements. Thus, you may amend your campaign
disclosure forms to reflect the accurate status of the payment as
a loan. Once this has been completed, you may repay the loan from
your campaign funds.

Please note, however, as discussed in our telephone conversa-
tion of January 31st, the repayment of the loan must be made to
the campaign bank account of the maker from which the loan was

___________________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory refer-
ences are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Com-
mission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. A-90-087
Page 2

made and from your campaign bank account to which the loan was
made. Since you do not currently have a campaign bank account and
committee for your 1988 campaign, you may establish a campaign
bank account for the purpose of raising and expending funds for
campaign debts incurred during your 1988 campaign for the As-
sembly. This is accomplished by filing a candidate intention
statement and campaign bank account statement (Forms 501 and 502)
to solicit and receive contributions for your election to the As-
sembly in 1988. Contributions may then be deposited in the 1988
Assembly campaign bank account for the purpose of paying debts.

Please keep in mind that all contributions to your 1988
campaign bank account must be aggregated on a fiscal year basis
with contributions to any other campaign bank accounts which you
control (e.g. your 1990 and any other future campaign bank ac-
counts), to determine when a contributor has reached the contribu-
tion limits of the Act.2/

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact this office at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
     Counsel, Legal Division

KED:JWW:plh


2/ Due to the preliminary injunction in Service Employees
International Union, AFL-CIO, et al. v. Fair Political Practices
Commission, United States District Court, Eastern District of
California, Case No. CIVS-89-0433, LKK-JFM, candidates may
transfer campaign funds among their own campaign bank accounts.
